behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (CSV row index tracking -> schema validator -> DLQ writer), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce; an in-process DLQ fake can expose wrong row-number propagation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-CHECK quotes: cross-module need: "appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number"; Unit disqualified because a single validator function cannot prove row number survives into DLQ payload; higher levels disqualified because behavior text does not require real S3/real PostgreSQL/restart/LLM. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System due behavior text focusing only on rejection + DLQ content, with no requirement for real network/OS.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline components together with an in-memory CSV source and DLQ sink: feed rows with missing required fields at known positions, assert rejected records in DLQ include exact original row numbers and valid rows continue. This catches boundary bugs where index metadata is dropped or rewritten between modules.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets parsing/normalization logic itself, which is a local transformation function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependence. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is strictly "transformation correctly converts date strings...", which is single-function logic and does not require module handoff to fail.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous and invalid dates), asserting canonical output or explicit rejection. This catches locale-order and parser-branch defects without broader pipeline wiring.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timeout and memory-exhaustion are OS/runtime behaviors; in-process unit/integration fakes can hide GC, buffering, and allocator pressure. MOCK-HIDE: mocked I/O and tiny fixtures can pass while real large-file streaming fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires "500,000 rows", "configured timeout", and "without running out of memory", which are real runtime constraints.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a large generated CSV (500k rows) with production-like config; measure wall-clock completion before timeout and assert peak memory stays below limit (or no OOM/kill). Fail if batch exceeds timeout or process memory growth is unbounded.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior references real S3 transient HTTP 503 semantics; minimum robust boundary is real HTTP interaction with S3-compatible endpoint and timing/backoff observation. MOCK-HIDE: simple stubs may not reproduce SDK error mapping/retryable classification and can falsely pass. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because phrase "S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error" describes external API behavior at network boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3-compatible test endpoint that injects transient 503 responses, then succeeds; assert retry count and backoff intervals grow exponentially and batch completes without full failure. This catches misclassified retryable errors and incorrect backoff scheduling.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires restart behavior plus persistent DB state across process lifecycle. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory doubles cannot faithfully expose crash/restart idempotency faults (offset checkpoint vs committed rows). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "restarted mid-batch" and depends on real persistence in "PostgreSQL" across restart boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline on a controlled batch with unique row IDs, force termination after partial commit, restart pipeline with same input and checkpoint state, then assert PostgreSQL contains each logical row exactly once (no duplicates) and processing resumes correctly. This catches non-atomic checkpoint/commit ordering bugs.
